articles.title: The AI code wars are heating up
articles.summary: AI coding tools are becoming increasingly competitive, with multiple platforms racing to dominate the code generation market. This trend highlights AI's significant impact on software development and programming practices.

articles.title: Your article about AI doesn’t need AI art
articles.summary: The New Yorker used AI-generated art for a Sam Altman profile illustration, crediting artist David Szauder. The disclosure has sparked concern among illustrators about AI's role in creative work.

articles.title: My baby deer plushie told me that Mitski’s dad was a CIA operative
articles.summary: A reviewer receives an unsolicited text from an AI companion plushie named Coral that spreads unverified claims about musician Mitski's father being a CIA operative, raising concerns about AI misinformation.

articles.title: From LLMs to hallucinations, here’s a simple guide to common AI terms
articles.summary: A glossary explaining common AI terminology emerging from the rise of artificial intelligence, including definitions of important words and phrases frequently encountered in AI discussions.

articles.title: At the HumanX conference, everyone was talking about Claude
articles.summary: Anthropic's Claude AI dominated discussions at San Francisco's HumanX conference, making it the standout attraction of the AI-centric event.

articles.title: Sam Altman responds to ‘incendiary’ New Yorker article after attack on his home
articles.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman addressed a New Yorker profile questioning his trustworthiness and an apparent attack on his home through a blog post response.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure, challenging AWS and Google Cloud. The platform has attracted 2 million developers by addressing limitations of legacy cloud solutions for AI applications.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges up to $200/month for AI coding assistance, sparking developer backlash. Goose, a free open-source alternative by Block, offers similar functionality locally without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring stunt in San Francisco. The startup decoded AI tokens into a coding challenge, attracting thousands of applicants and successfully recruiting talent.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims to have migrated approximately 30,000 VMware customers due to negative sentiment surrounding Broadcom's acquisition and management of VMware.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iran-linked hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure by targeting programmable logic controllers at factories, water treatment centers, and oil refineries. Six US government agencies issued urgent warnings about the advanced persistent threat group's attacks since March 2026.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russian military's APT28 hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers globally, redirecting users to credential-stealing sites for espionage operations targeting government and intelligence agencies.

articles.title: What’s in a name? Moderna’s “vaccine” vs. “therapy” dilemma
articles.summary: Moderna faces vaccine skepticism in the Trump administration, losing funding for mRNA vaccine programs. The company pivots to rebranding its technology as individualized treatments to bypass regulatory obstacles.

articles.title: The Download: an exclusive Jeff VanderMeer story and AI models too scary to release
articles.summary: Jeff VanderMeer's short story features a spacecraft crash-landed on a hostile alien planet where three survivors and an AI must navigate mysterious domes connected by cables for survival.

articles.title: Constellations
articles.summary: A crash-landed crew on a hostile alien planet must explore to survive. They discover alien artifacts nearby, offering hope despite their dire situation.

articles.title: Waypoint-1.5: Higher-Fidelity Interactive Worlds for Everyday GPUs
articles.summary: Waypoint-1.5 enables creation of high-fidelity interactive 3D worlds on standard consumer GPUs. The advancement makes complex virtual environment development more accessible to everyday users.

articles.title: Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
articles.summary: Sentence Transformers now supports multimodal embedding and reranker models, enabling processing of text and images together for improved semantic understanding and ranking tasks.

articles.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
articles.summary: ALTK-Evolve enables AI agents to learn and improve continuously during task execution rather than relying solely on pre-training. The system allows agents to adapt and enhance performance through on-the-job experience.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its developments. The conversation explores what innovations and advancements are expected in the AI field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing strategies.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft open-sources its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural AI technology publicly available. The release aims to advance sustainable farming practices through accessible technology.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces two new Gemini API inference tiers—Flex and Priority—to balance cost and latency for users.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI-powered video creation and editing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1, enabling high-quality video generation at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to protect and monitor the country's forests.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live voice model improves precision and reduces latency for more natural, fluid voice interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches how AI can harmfully manipulate people in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to mitigate risks.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, a generative AI graphics technology that enhances video game photorealism in real-time up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate detailed frames.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and Cursor's trigger-based coding automations. ChatGPT and Anthropic introduced interactive visual features.

articles.title: IBM: How robust AI governance protects enterprise margins
articles.summary: IBM emphasizes that robust AI governance is essential for enterprises to protect margins and securely manage AI infrastructure as it evolves from standalone product to foundational infrastructure.

articles.title: Why companies like Apple are building AI agents with limits
articles.summary: Apple and Qualcomm are developing AI agents with built-in safety limits, requiring user confirmation for sensitive actions like payments. These assistants can navigate apps and complete tasks but use a human-in-the-loop approach.

articles.title: Meta has a competitive AI model but loses its open-source identity
articles.summary: Meta launched Muse Spark, a proprietary AI model that outperforms Llama 4, marking a shift from its open-source commitment despite Llama's massive 1.2 billion downloads by early 2026.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, advancing understanding of collaborative AI problem-solving.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses core challenges like localization and path planning in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering systems built around them are emerging as the true competitive advantage, as evidenced by Anthropic's Claude Code leak.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to harvest credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 model advances video self-supervised learning by successfully balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, overcoming a major challenge in AI development.

articles.title: MiniMax Just Open Sourced MiniMax M2.7: A Self-Evolving Agent Model that Scores 56.22% on SWE-Pro and 57.0% on Terminal Bench 2
articles.summary: MiniMax open-sourced M2.7, a self-evolving agent model using Mixture-of-Experts architecture. It scores 56.22% on SWE-Pro and 57.0% on Terminal Bench 2, excelling in software engineering and office work.

articles.title: Liquid AI Releases LFM2.5-VL-450M: a 450M-Parameter Vision-Language Model with Bounding Box Prediction, Multilingual Support, and Sub-250ms Edge Inference
articles.summary: Liquid AI released LFM2.5-VL-450M, a 450M-parameter vision-language model with bounding box prediction, multilingual support, and function calling. It runs on edge devices with sub-250ms inference speed.

articles.title: Researchers from MIT, NVIDIA, and Zhejiang University Propose TriAttention: A KV Cache Compression Method That Matches Full Attention at 2.5× Higher Throughput
articles.summary: Researchers propose TriAttention, a KV cache compression method that maintains full attention accuracy while achieving 2.5× higher throughput and 10.7× memory reduction for long-chain reasoning tasks.

articles.title: 30 years later, I returned to Enlightenment Linux to test the Elive beta - and it's much better
articles.summary: Elive, a Debian-based distro, revives the classic Enlightenment desktop environment with modernized UI. The beta shows improvement but still lacks some features.

articles.title: Here's my favorite email trick for cleaning up inbox clutter - automatically
articles.summary: A built-in email feature can automatically clean up inbox clutter from ads, newsletters, and social media updates, helping organize overflowing inboxes efficiently.

articles.title: After using Lenovo's new Yoga laptop, I'm wondering if Windows makers are running out of ideas
articles.summary: Lenovo's new Yoga 7a 2-in-1 is versatile and well-built, but its mid-tier hardware struggles to compete with rivals, raising questions about Windows PC innovation.

articles.title: Beyond Vector Search: Building a Deterministic 3-Tiered Graph-RAG System
articles.summary: This article discusses building a deterministic 3-tiered Graph-RAG system that goes beyond traditional vector search methods. It presents an advanced approach for improved retrieval-augmented generation using graph structures.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Agentic AI Design Patterns
articles.summary: Article outlines design patterns for building effective agentic AI systems. Covers fundamental approaches to creating autonomous AI agents.

articles.title: A Hands-On Guide to Testing Agents with RAGAs and G-Eval
articles.summary: A practical guide for testing AI agents using RAGAS and G-Eval evaluation frameworks. Learn how to assess agent performance and reliability through hands-on testing methodologies.

articles.title: Stop Treating AI Memory Like a Search Problem
articles.summary: AI memory systems require more than simple data storage and retrieval mechanisms. Building reliable memory demands rethinking how AI systems organize and access information.

articles.title: Write Pandas Like a Pro With Method Chaining Pipelines
articles.summary: Learn method chaining, assign(), and pipe() techniques to write cleaner, more testable, production-ready Pandas code that separates intermediate users from beginners.

articles.title: Your ReAct Agent Is Wasting 90% of Its Retries — Here’s How to Stop It
articles.summary: ReAct agents waste 90% of retries on hallucinated tool calls due to architectural flaws, not model errors. Three structural changes can eliminate wasted retries entirely.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: NVIDIA highlights physical AI breakthroughs during National Robotics Week, showcasing how robots transform industries. Advances in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models enable faster real-world deployment.

articles.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
articles.summary: Samson: A Tyndalston Story by Liquid Swords is now available on GeForce NOW, allowing players to stream the mythic adventure about faith and rebellion on nearly any device.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for efficient local execution on various devices, from RTX PCs to data centers, enabling on-device agentic AI with real-time context processing.

articles.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock regularly releases improved foundation model versions. Understanding the model lifecycle is crucial for managing AI applications and testing new models before migration.

articles.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
articles.summary: AWS Agent Registry, now in preview through Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, enables enterprises to discover, share, and reuse agents and tools across their organization, addressing challenges of visibility, control, and preventing duplicate development work at scale.

articles.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore introduces BrowserLiveView, a React component enabling real-time visibility of AI agents navigating websites. It embeds live browser streams with just three lines of JSX code.

articles.title: MiniMax M2.7 Advances Scalable Agentic Workflows on NVIDIA Platforms for Complex AI Applications
articles.summary: MiniMax M2.7 enhances the M2.5 model with improved agentic workflow capabilities on NVIDIA platforms, designed for complex applications in reasoning, ML research, software engineering, and office automation.

articles.title: Running Large-Scale GPU Workloads on Kubernetes with Slurm
articles.summary: Slurm, an open-source cluster management system used by 65% of TOP500 systems, enables efficient GPU workload scheduling on Kubernetes. Organizations can leverage existing Slurm investments and job scripts for large-scale AI training.

articles.title: Cut Checkpoint Costs with About 30 Lines of Python and NVIDIA nvCOMP
articles.summary: NVIDIA's nvCOMP enables LLM checkpoint compression using just 30 lines of Python, significantly reducing storage costs for massive model snapshots during training.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how generational differences shape consumer attitudes toward social media advertising across various platforms, revealing insights into people's relationships with ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon footprint mitigation technology and sharing it openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background pioneered innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how unconventional paths can lead to breakthrough innovations in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers present methods for identifying feature interactions in Large Language Models at scale to improve interpretability and understand model decision-making processes for safer AI.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents an information-driven approach to design imaging systems that optimize how well measurements distinguish objects. The method uses noisy measurements and noise models to quantify imaging system performance.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, addressing TD's scalability issues and performing better on long-horizon tasks.
